infuse 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • infuse: A Quick Start Guide

    Map Values to Empirical Distribution Steps (Strictly Less Than)

    c_ecdf_values_less.Rd

    Evaluates a step function (like an ECDF) at specific points using the logic \(P(X < z)\). This results in a left-continuous function, where the value at a jump point is the value of the lower step.

    c_ecdf_values_less(uni, w, z, ofset = 0)

    Arguments

    uni

    A sorted numeric vector of unique time points or breakpoints.

    w

    A numeric vector of weights or probabilities associated with each interval.

    z

    A numeric vector of points at which the function should be evaluated.

    ofset

    A numeric value subtracted from each element of z. Defaults to 0.

    Value

    A numeric vector of the same length as z containing the mapped values from w.

    Details

    This function uses std::lower_bound to find the first index where uni >= target. Consequently, it maps to the weight associated with values strictly less than the target.

    For a given target \(t = z_i - \text{ofset}\), the result is:

    • 0 if \(t \le \text{uni}[0]\)

    • \(w[j-1]\) where \(j\) is the index such that \(\text{uni}[j-1] < t \le \text{uni}[j]\)

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Standard ECDF (X <= z) vs Strict ECDF (X < z)
times <- c(1, 3, 5)
weights <- c(0.2, 0.5, 0.9)

# At the jump point (z = 3):
# c_ecdf_values would return 0.5
# c_ecdf_values_less returns 0.2
c_ecdf_values_less(times, weights, 3, ofset = 0)
} # }

    Contents
